narrative: On 16/03/2025 at about 11:00 AM the complainant left his house at Sihyavada, Sanand locked. Around 9:00 PM the complainant's wife discovered the lock of the front door broken. Inside the house, the wooden patara had been opened and the small steel box kept inside it had been broken open. Cash Rs. 50,000 and gold and silver ornaments worth Rs. 2,26,500 were found stolen from the steel box. Suspicion is recorded against the house-helper Asalambhai Bhikhabhai Muslim, resident of Moti Kadechi, Taluka Limbdi, District Surendranagar.
rationale_facts: lock of door broken to gain entry
ai_curator_notes: Reviewed without changes; sub-clause-precise and complete on the FIR facts as written.

narrative: On 27/01/2025 at about 9:00 PM to 9:30 PM at Balapir Dargah, Viramgam, the complainant intervened in a settlement matter. Three accused — Rajubhai, Anubhai and Sanjubhai (all Amratbhai Devipujak) — arrived. Sanjubhai and Anubhai grabbed the complainant and held him firmly while Rajubhai struck him twice on the head with a wooden stick. The complainant sustained a bleeding head wound and was taken to Sara Gandhi Hospital for treatment. The accused threatened to cut off the complainant's nose and warned him not to intervene in the settlement.
rationale_facts: wooden stick used to strike head
criminal intimidation by threat of grievous hurt
ai_curator_notes: Reviewed without changes; sub-clause-precise and complete on the FIR facts as written.

narrative: On 12/08/2024 at about 2:00 AM the complainant residing at Naranpura, Ahmedabad heard noises from the kitchen. On checking he saw two unknown persons who had entered the house through a window after breaking the grill. Seeing the complainant, the accused fled with a gold chain and a wristwatch worth approximately Rs. 60,000.
rationale_facts: broken window grill — housebreaking by night
ai_curator_notes: Reviewed without changes; sub-clause-precise and complete on the FIR facts as written.

narrative: On 03/09/2024 at about 7:30 PM the complainant was riding his motorcycle on the Sarkhej-Gandhinagar road when an unknown person on a moving motorcycle suddenly came alongside, snatched the complainant's mobile phone from his hand and sped away. The phone is worth approximately Rs. 18,000.
rationale_facts: snatching from a person — sudden, quick, forcible taking
ai_curator_notes: Reviewed without changes; sub-clause-precise and complete on the FIR facts as written.

narrative: On 22/10/2024 at about 11:00 PM at Maninagar bus stand, six accused armed with sticks and knives surrounded the complainant. They threatened him with death if he did not hand over his belongings. The accused snatched cash Rs. 12,000, a mobile phone, and a gold chain. They beat the complainant with sticks causing simple injuries to his arms and legs.
ai_curator_notes: Upgraded BNS 310 → BNS 310(2) for sub-clause precision (the operative punishment limb for dacoity).

narrative: On 18/11/2024 the complainant runs a small business. Accused Mukeshbhai, who was entrusted with daily collection of cash from the shop and depositing it in the bank, has been collecting cash for the past three months but has not deposited Rs. 4,50,000 into the bank account. On verification with the bank, no deposits have been made. The accused has admitted to using the funds for personal purposes.
ai_curator_notes: Reviewed without changes; sub-clause-precise and complete on the FIR facts as written.

narrative: On 05/12/2024 the complainant entered into an agreement to purchase a flat from accused Rajeshbhai for Rs. 35,00,000. Complainant paid Rs. 12,00,000 as advance. Accused had already sold the same flat to another buyer one month before this agreement and concealed this fact. Accused used the complainant's money knowing the property was not available to be sold. Accused has refused to return the money.
ai_curator_notes: Reviewed without changes; sub-clause-precise and complete on the FIR facts as written.

narrative: On 14/01/2025 at about 4:30 PM at the local market, accused Sureshbhai approached the complainant after a verbal dispute and in front of witnesses spoke loudly that the complainant is a thief who has stolen from his shop. These statements are false. As a result of these statements, the complainant has lost business and respect in the community.
ai_curator_notes: Reviewed without changes; sub-clause-precise and complete on the FIR facts as written.

narrative: On 02/02/2025 at about 8:00 PM the complainant residing at Vastrapur was followed by an unknown person on a motorcycle from her workplace to her residence. The same person has been following her for the past two weeks despite being asked to stop. He sends repeated messages on social media and telephone calls.
ai_curator_notes: Reviewed without changes; sub-clause-precise and complete on the FIR facts as written.

narrative: On 07/03/2025 at about 10:00 PM accused Kamleshbhai, the husband of the complainant, returned home in an intoxicated state and demanded additional dowry of Rs. 5,00,000. When the complainant refused, the accused beat her with belt and slapped her several times. The complainant has been subjected to similar harassment and demands for dowry on multiple occasions over the past two years.
ai_curator_notes: Reviewed without changes; sub-clause-precise and complete on the FIR facts as written.

narrative: On 19/04/2025 at about 6:00 PM at the public square in Bopal, two accused — Vinodbhai and Pravinbhai — engaged in a heated argument that escalated into a fistfight. They struck each other multiple times. Public gathered. The fight disturbed public peace until police arrived.
ai_curator_notes: Removed BNS 3(5). Affray is by definition a MUTUAL fight — both parties attack each other; common intention does not attach because there is no common object aimed at a third party.

narrative: On 11/05/2025 at about 3:00 PM accused Bharatbhai approached the complainant near her residence and made obscene gestures. He used vulgar words referring to her in a sexual manner. He also touched her shoulder without consent.
rationale_facts: sexual harassment by words and physical contact
ai_curator_notes: Added BNS 74 (assault or use of criminal force to woman with intent to outrage modesty) — the accused touched her shoulder without consent. Touch + sexual gestures attracts 74 in addition to verbal harassment under 75(2) and 79.

narrative: On 06/06/2025 at about 11:30 AM accused Maheshbhai and Rakeshbhai forcibly entered the complainant's residence after threatening the security guard. They demanded that the complainant withdraw a civil complaint pending against them. They damaged the television and broke window panes worth Rs. 35,000.
rationale_facts: intimidation to compel withdrawal of complaint
mischief — damage to property worth Rs. 35,000
ai_curator_notes: Reviewed without changes; sub-clause-precise and complete on the FIR facts as written.

narrative: On 23/07/2025 at about 1:00 AM the complainant's car was stolen from outside his residence at Naranpura. The car is a Honda City registration GJ-01-AB-1234, valued at Rs. 7,00,000. The car was locked. The accused are unknown.
rationale_facts: theft of a means of transport
ai_curator_notes: Reviewed without changes; sub-clause-precise and complete on the FIR facts as written.

narrative: On 04/08/2025 at about 5:00 AM the priest at the Shri Ramji Mandir, Maninagar discovered that the silver idol of the deity worth Rs. 1,75,000 had been stolen from the temple. The lock of the temple gate was found broken. The accused are unknown.
rationale_facts: theft of an idol from a place of worship
ai_curator_notes: Added BNS 332 Provided that — house-trespass with intent to commit theft (the proviso extends punishment to 7 years where intended offence is theft).

narrative: On 29/08/2025 at about 9:00 PM the complainant was returning home alone when accused Anilbhai approached him with a knife and demanded all valuables. The accused threatened to stab if the complainant did not comply. The complainant handed over Rs. 8,000 cash, a gold ring and a mobile phone. After taking the items the accused fled.
rationale_facts: robbery — taking property by putting in fear of instant hurt
ai_curator_notes: Reviewed without changes; sub-clause-precise and complete on the FIR facts as written.

narrative: On 13/09/2025 at about 4:00 PM the complainant received an SMS purportedly from his bank stating that his account would be blocked unless he provided his OTP. The complainant disclosed the OTP. Subsequently Rs. 1,85,000 was withdrawn from his account in three transactions to an unknown account. Bank records confirm the withdrawal.
rationale_facts: cheating by deception (false SMS impersonating bank)
fraudulent inducement to part with property (OTP)
ai_curator_notes: Reviewed without changes; sub-clause-precise and complete on the FIR facts as written.

narrative: On 25/09/2025 at about 10:00 AM accused Vijaybhai forged the signature of the complainant on a property sale deed and used the forged document to obtain a loan of Rs. 8,00,000 from a bank. The forgery has been confirmed by the bank's verification team.
rationale_facts: use of forged document as genuine
cheating by deception of the bank
ai_curator_notes: Reviewed without changes; sub-clause-precise and complete on the FIR facts as written.

narrative: On 09/10/2025 at about 6:00 PM accused Pradeepbhai who is a public servant in the local revenue office demanded a bribe of Rs. 50,000 from the complainant for issuing a routine land record. The complainant refused. The accused threatened that the work would not be done without payment.
rationale_facts: public servant disobeying law with intent to cause injury
demand for bribe (consider Prevention of Corruption Act for primary charge)
ai_curator_notes: Added BNS 351(2) — the accused 'threatened that the work would not be done without payment' — meets criminal-intimidation standard. Note: the primary charge for bribery should be under the Prevention of Corruption Act, 1988; flagged for SME on inter-act allocation.

narrative: On 17/10/2025 at about 8:00 PM accused Manojbhai, while driving his car at high speed in a residential area, hit a pedestrian causing fatal injuries. The pedestrian died on the spot. Eyewitnesses state the accused was driving rashly and ignored a red signal.
rationale_facts: death caused by rash and negligent driving
ai_curator_notes: Reviewed without changes; sub-clause-precise and complete on the FIR facts as written.

narrative: On 04/11/2024 at about 1:30 PM the complainant was driving his scooter when accused Vipulbhai, driving a car rashly on the wrong side of the road, collided with the complainant. The complainant sustained fractures to his right leg. He was admitted to hospital for one week. MLC has been obtained classifying the injuries as grievous.
rationale_facts: grievous hurt by act endangering life
rash driving on wrong side of road
ai_curator_notes: Reviewed without changes; sub-clause-precise and complete on the FIR facts as written.

narrative: On 19/12/2024 at about 11:00 AM at the railway station, accused Praful pickpocketed the complainant's wallet containing Rs. 6,500 cash, identity cards, and bank cards. The complainant noticed the theft when he reached for his wallet to buy a ticket. CCTV footage identifies the accused.
rationale_facts: pickpocketing — covert taking from a person's pocket
ai_curator_notes: Reviewed without changes; sub-clause-precise and complete on the FIR facts as written.

narrative: On 28/12/2024 at about 11:30 PM at the complainant's home in Vasna, the accused Sunilbhai, who is a known acquaintance, came in an intoxicated state and demanded money. When refused, he produced a kitchen knife and slashed the complainant's forearm causing a 4-inch laceration that required hospital sutures. The accused fled. MLC obtained.
ai_curator_notes: Reviewed without changes; sub-clause-precise and complete on the FIR facts as written.

narrative: On 11/01/2025 at about 6:00 AM the complainant discovered her newborn baby missing from the home. The baby was last seen at midnight. Investigation reveals that the housemaid Geetaben kidnapped the baby. The maid had been working at the house for two months. The baby has not been recovered.
rationale_facts: kidnapping of a child below 10 years of age
ai_curator_notes: Removed BNS 139 (kidnapping/abducting in order to murder or dispose of body) — the FIR shows no murder intent; the maid kidnapped the baby without any indication of intent to kill. 139 was over-charged.

narrative: On 24/01/2025 at about 9:00 PM at Bhuyangdev Char Rasta, four accused armed with iron rods and a country-made pistol confronted the complainant who was driving a tempo. They brandished the pistol, beat the complainant with rods, snatched cash Rs. 75,000 collected from the day's business, and the tempo's keys. The complainant sustained simple injuries.
ai_curator_notes: Reviewed without changes; sub-clause-precise and complete on the FIR facts as written.

narrative: On 09/02/2025 at about 4:00 AM accused Hareshbhai set fire to the complainant's standing wheat crop in his agricultural field at Mansa, Gandhinagar. The fire destroyed crops worth Rs. 3,75,000. The complainant suspects the accused due to a long-standing land dispute. Eyewitnesses saw the accused running from the field carrying a kerosene can.
ai_curator_notes: Reviewed without changes; sub-clause-precise and complete on the FIR facts as written.

narrative: On 21/02/2025 at about 7:30 PM accused Pintubhai sold an ointment claimed to cure cancer to the complainant for Rs. 25,000. The accused represented himself as a qualified doctor with a clinic. He had no medical qualifications. The complainant later learned that no such clinic existed and the ointment is harmful.
ai_curator_notes: Reviewed without changes; sub-clause-precise and complete on the FIR facts as written.

narrative: On 13/03/2025 at about 5:00 PM at the complainant's office, accused Ravibhai who is the complainant's former employee returned and assaulted him with a chair, causing a head injury that required 8 stitches. The accused was angry over termination of employment. The injury is documented in MLC as simple hurt.
rationale_facts: chair used as weapon — likely to cause hurt
ai_curator_notes: Reviewed without changes; sub-clause-precise and complete on the FIR facts as written.

narrative: On 28/03/2025 at about 10:00 PM accused Faridbhai entered the complainant's shop, abused her with caste-based slurs, and threatened to harm her if she did not vacate the shop. The complainant belongs to the Scheduled Caste community.
rationale_facts: caste-based abuse (also consider SC/ST Atrocities Act for primary charge)
ai_curator_notes: Reviewed without changes; sub-clause-precise and complete on the FIR facts as written.

narrative: On 09/04/2025 at about 2:00 PM accused Naileshbhai impersonated a police officer in uniform and demanded Rs. 30,000 from the complainant claiming there was an arrest warrant. The complainant paid the amount before discovering that the accused was not a real police officer.
ai_curator_notes: Reviewed without changes; sub-clause-precise and complete on the FIR facts as written.

narrative: On 17/04/2025 at about 11:00 PM accused Vishal forcibly poured kerosene on the complainant's wife and set her on fire over a dowry dispute. The wife sustained 60% burns and died on the way to hospital. The accused has been arrested. Post-mortem confirms death by burn injuries.
rationale_facts: dowry death within 7 years of marriage
ai_curator_notes: Added BNS 85 (cruelty by husband or relative). The narrative establishes a pattern of dowry harassment culminating in the burning. 80 + 103 + 85 are routinely charged together in dowry-death cases at FIR stage; courts then choose at framing.

narrative: On 03/05/2025 at about 3:00 PM accused Devang created a fake social media profile of the complainant and posted intimate images obtained from her phone after a relationship ended. The images have been viewed by hundreds of people. The complainant has filed for take-down.
rationale_facts: voyeurism — circulation of intimate images without consent
consider IT Act sections for primary charge
ai_curator_notes: Reviewed without changes; sub-clause-precise and complete on the FIR facts as written.

narrative: On 18/05/2025 at about 6:00 AM the complainant who is a journalist received a death threat over phone. The caller said the complainant would be killed if he did not stop reporting on a particular politician. Phone records have been preserved.
rationale_facts: criminal intimidation by threat of death
ai_curator_notes: Reviewed without changes; sub-clause-precise and complete on the FIR facts as written.

narrative: On 02/06/2025 at about 9:00 PM accused Nileshbhai administered intoxicating substance in the complainant's drink at a house party. The complainant lost consciousness. When she regained consciousness, she discovered her gold jewellery and cash had been stolen.
rationale_facts: administering stupefying substance with intent to commit offence
ai_curator_notes: Added BNS 303(2) — the punishment limb for theft. 305(a) covers the dwelling-context aggravation; 303(2) is the underlying offence.

narrative: On 14/06/2025 at about 8:00 PM at a public function, accused Mehulbhai tried to grab the complainant who is a 6-year-old girl. The accused was caught by family members before he could harm the child. The accused has been previously known for similar attempts.
rationale_facts: attempted kidnapping of a minor (also consider POCSO for primary charge)
ai_curator_notes: Added BNS 62 (attempt to commit offence). The kidnapping was attempted, not completed — the accused was caught before he could harm. Without 62, the unfinished nature of the act isn't reflected. POCSO Act remains the primary surface for child-victim cases.

narrative: On 27/06/2025 at about 10:30 AM accused Suniltai who is a contractor took an advance of Rs. 1,75,000 from the complainant for construction work. The work has not commenced after six months. The accused has been avoiding the complainant and refusing to return the money. Multiple reminders sent.
ai_curator_notes: Reviewed without changes; sub-clause-precise and complete on the FIR facts as written.

narrative: On 08/07/2025 at about 2:00 AM accused Praveen and Manish broke open the back door of the jewellery shop, stole gold ornaments worth Rs. 12,50,000 and cash Rs. 80,000 from the cash drawer. CCTV captured both accused. The shop is locked overnight with metal shutters.
rationale_facts: theft from a building used for custody of property
back door broken — housebreaking by night
ai_curator_notes: Reviewed without changes; sub-clause-precise and complete on the FIR facts as written.

narrative: On 22/07/2025 at about 7:00 PM at a marriage function in Mehsana, the complainant who is the bride's brother was attacked by 8 persons from the groom's side over a dispute about gifts. They beat him with sticks causing injuries to his head, arms and ribs. The complainant lost consciousness. MLC confirms three rib fractures and a head injury — grievous.
rationale_facts: grievous hurt — rib fractures and head injury per MLC
ai_curator_notes: Added BNS 189 (unlawful-assembly definition) so the unlawful-assembly chain is complete: 189 (defines) + 190 (every member liable) + 191 (rioting). Removed BNS 3(5) — for 5+ accused, joint liability is attributed via 190 specifically; 3(5) and 190 are conceptually overlapping and should not be charged together to avoid double-attribution objections.

narrative: On 04/08/2025 at about 11:00 AM accused Devyani who is the complainant's neighbour entered the complainant's house when no one was home and opened cupboards. She was caught by the complainant's daughter who returned unexpectedly. No items were taken. The accused fled.
rationale_facts: house-trespass with intent to commit offence — theft
ai_curator_notes: Reviewed without changes; sub-clause-precise and complete on the FIR facts as written.

narrative: On 19/08/2025 at about 8:00 PM the complainant's wife who is pregnant was beaten by her mother-in-law and sister-in-law over a domestic dispute. The complainant's wife sustained injuries to face and abdomen. MLC obtained showing simple injuries but a risk to the pregnancy.
ai_curator_notes: Reviewed without changes; sub-clause-precise and complete on the FIR facts as written.

narrative: On 02/09/2025 at about 3:00 AM accused Sanjay who was the complainant's tenant absconded with the LED TV, washing machine, and microwave from the rental flat without giving notice or paying outstanding rent of Rs. 65,000. The items belong to the complainant.
rationale_facts: theft from a dwelling at night
ai_curator_notes: Reviewed without changes; sub-clause-precise and complete on the FIR facts as written.

narrative: On 16/09/2025 at about 12:30 PM accused Hitesh who runs a coaching centre charged Rs. 80,000 from the complainant's son for IIT preparation classes. The accused has shut down the centre after one month and is not refunding the fees. He had no qualifications to run such classes and many other students have been similarly defrauded.
ai_curator_notes: Reviewed without changes; sub-clause-precise and complete on the FIR facts as written.

narrative: On 30/09/2025 at about 6:00 AM accused Bhaveshbhai assaulted his elderly father aged 78 years over property dispute. The father sustained a fractured wrist and bruises on the back. MLC confirms grievous hurt.
rationale_facts: grievous hurt — wrist fracture per MLC
ai_curator_notes: Reviewed without changes; sub-clause-precise and complete on the FIR facts as written.

narrative: On 14/10/2025 at about 9:00 PM accused Manjula who is the complainant's domestic help left the house carrying jewellery worth Rs. 4,25,000 from the complainant's almirah. The almirah was unlocked. Manjula has not returned to work and her phone is switched off.
rationale_facts: by a person having lawful access (servant)
ai_curator_notes: Reviewed without changes; sub-clause-precise and complete on the FIR facts as written.

narrative: On 28/10/2025 at about 4:00 PM accused Roopesh and Kamal grabbed the complainant who is a 14-year-old boy from outside his school and forced him into a car. They demanded ransom of Rs. 25,00,000 from the parents. The boy was rescued by police 3 days later.
ai_curator_notes: Reviewed without changes; sub-clause-precise and complete on the FIR facts as written.

narrative: On 11/11/2025 at about 10:00 PM accused Jignesh who is the complainant's neighbour entered the complainant's vacant flat using a duplicate key obtained from the watchman, stole electronic items worth Rs. 1,55,000 including a laptop and gaming console.
rationale_facts: entry by means other than ordinary access — duplicate key
ai_curator_notes: Reviewed without changes; sub-clause-precise and complete on the FIR facts as written.

narrative: On 25/11/2025 at about 7:00 PM accused Mukund harassed the complainant who is a young woman returning home from work. He followed her on a motorcycle for several kilometres, made gestures and shouted indecent comments. The complainant noted his vehicle number.
ai_curator_notes: Reviewed without changes; sub-clause-precise and complete on the FIR facts as written.

narrative: On 09/12/2025 at about 1:00 AM accused Ramesh stole copper electrical cables worth Rs. 2,80,000 from the GETCO substation by climbing the boundary wall and cutting the cables. He was caught by security.
rationale_facts: theft of property of local authority/government
ai_curator_notes: Reviewed without changes; sub-clause-precise and complete on the FIR facts as written.

narrative: On 23/12/2025 at about 6:30 PM accused Pravinbhai poured petrol on his wife after a quarrel and threatened to set her on fire. He was prevented by neighbours. The wife sustained no burns but suffered shock.
rationale_facts: criminal intimidation by threat of death
attempt to commit murder by fire — preparation made
ai_curator_notes: Sub-clause precision: BNS 109 → BNS 109(1) (the operative limb for attempt-to-murder with intent or knowledge that would constitute murder if death ensued). Added BNS 85 (cruelty by husband) — the act arose 'after a quarrel' between husband and wife in domestic context.

narrative: On 06/01/2026 at about 11:00 AM accused Pranav, the complainant's business partner, removed Rs. 8,50,000 from the joint business account and transferred it to his personal account without authorisation. He has admitted the transfer but refuses to return.
ai_curator_notes: Reviewed without changes; sub-clause-precise and complete on the FIR facts as written.

narrative: On 19/01/2026 at about 9:30 PM accused Kishorbhai stabbed the complainant in a road-rage incident with a screwdriver. The complainant sustained an abdominal puncture wound that endangered life. Emergency surgery was performed. MLC classifies as grievous endangering life.
rationale_facts: grievous hurt endangering life — abdominal stab
attempt to commit murder per facts
ai_curator_notes: Sub-clause precision: BNS 109 → BNS 109(1). The abdominal stab endangering life with surgical intervention is textbook attempt-to-murder facts.

narrative: On 02/02/2026 at about 4:00 AM accused Nikhilbhai broke open the lock of the complainant's two-wheeler and stole the Activa scooter parked outside the apartment. CCTV captured the theft. The vehicle is registered as GJ-05-CD-5678.
ai_curator_notes: Reviewed without changes; sub-clause-precise and complete on the FIR facts as written.

narrative: On 16/02/2026 at about 5:00 PM the complainant lent Rs. 3,00,000 to accused Hareshbhai based on a fabricated medical emergency story for his son. There was no such emergency. The money was used by the accused for personal purposes. The accused refuses to repay.
ai_curator_notes: Reviewed without changes; sub-clause-precise and complete on the FIR facts as written.

narrative: On 28/02/2026 at about 8:00 PM accused Bhupesh and Dinesh together damaged the complainant's parked car with cricket bats. The car (Innova) sustained dents on the bonnet, broken side mirrors, and a smashed windshield. Damage estimated at Rs. 1,25,000. Witnesses present.
ai_curator_notes: Sub-clause re-tier: damage of ₹1,25,000 exceeds the ₹1,00,000 threshold of BNS 324(4) and falls within BNS 324(5) (≥ ₹1 lakh, up to 5 years).

narrative: On 13/03/2026 at about 2:00 PM accused Bharatbhai intentionally fed his neighbour's pet dog with poisoned meat. The dog died. The complainant has the post-mortem report. The accused has been heard saying he wanted to teach the neighbour a lesson.
ai_curator_notes: Added BNS 324(2) (basic mischief) alongside BNS 325. The applicability of BNS 325 (mischief by killing 'useful animal') to a domestic pet is not free of doubt — mischief-by-poisoning of property (the pet) is indisputable, so 324(2) is the safer floor charge. Flag for SME panel: position on 'pet as useful animal' under BNS 325.

narrative: On 27/03/2026 at about 11:30 PM accused Sandeep brought 9 friends to the complainant's house and forcefully entered. They beat the complainant and his wife with sticks, abused them with caste-based slurs, damaged household items worth Rs. 75,000, and stole cash Rs. 35,000. Complainant belongs to Scheduled Caste community.
rationale_facts: consider SC/ST Atrocities Act for primary
ai_curator_notes: Added BNS 189 to complete the unlawful-assembly chain. Removed BNS 3(5) — duplicative with BNS 190 for unlawful-assembly cases (see GS_0038 rationale). SC/ST Atrocities Act remains the primary chargeable surface for the caste element.

narrative: On 09/04/2026 at about 10:00 AM accused Vishalbhai who is a doctor performed an unauthorised surgical procedure on the complainant resulting in permanent loss of vision in one eye. The complainant alleges medical negligence and unauthorised consent. Medical board confirms loss of vision.
rationale_facts: grievous hurt — permanent loss of sight per BNS 116(b)
act endangering life or causing permanent injury
ai_curator_notes: Reviewed without changes; sub-clause-precise and complete on the FIR facts as written.

narrative: On 18/04/2026 at about 7:00 PM the complainant who works at a bank received a forged cheque of Rs. 5,75,000 deposited into the complainant's branch by accused Ramesh. The signature was forged. The forgery has been confirmed by the bank's verification cell. The accused was traced from CCTV.
ai_curator_notes: Reviewed without changes; sub-clause-precise and complete on the FIR facts as written.

narrative: On 03/05/2026 at about 9:00 AM accused Saurabh attempted to commit suicide by jumping in front of a moving train at Vatva Junction. He was pulled away by railway staff before the train arrived. He has admitted the attempt was due to financial stress.
ai_curator_notes: Reviewed without changes; sub-clause-precise and complete on the FIR facts as written.

narrative: On 17/05/2026 at about 5:30 PM accused Kunalbhai gave false witness statement before a magistrate's court regarding a property dispute. The accused stated under oath that he was present at a particular meeting, but documentary evidence (CCTV footage) shows he was not present. He has admitted the lie under cross-examination.
rationale_facts: giving false evidence in judicial proceeding
ai_curator_notes: Reviewed without changes; sub-clause-precise and complete on the FIR facts as written.
